programme.title: The Spirit of Zhaxidele
programme.desc: In this episode of Travelogue, Tianran backpacks the way to the western reaches of China. Making his home in beautiful Jiaju village, he learns just what it takes to be a Tibetan, then reaches the mysterious village of Suopo and explore its ancient watchtowers.

programme.title: Young drummer of Ansai
programme.desc: Soaring leaps and the energetic beats of the Ansai waist drum dance made Gao Yuchen, a nine-year-old boy, fall in love with the dance at first sight. Growing up in Ansai, Shaanxi, Gao was first drawn to the large-scale folk dance while watching a local performance. As a form of intangible cultural heritage, the Ansai waist drum dance was promoted by local authorities and was included as part of his school's PE program. Gao was able to receive waist drum dance training as an extracurricular activity at his school, and he subsequently became a rising star on campus. He says he wants to become a better dancer and hopes to perform on bigger stages.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Taiji Quan
programme.desc: Kleber travels to Chenjiagou in central China, the birthplace of Chen-style Taiji Quan. Also known as tai chi, the martial art is iconic for its slow movements and purported health benefits, but Kleber quickly discovers it’s the real deal: From being thrown around by teenagers to learning about the martial art’s Yin-Yang philosophy, Kleber experiences for himself how this kungfu style allows small to defeat big, and soft to beat strong.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-4 Medieval Combat
programme.desc: Episode 4 centres on Shanghai, where Eastern and Western martial traditions meet in surprising ways. Kleber visits local practitioners of Historical European Martial Arts (HEMA), trying German and Italian longsword techniques first-hand and seeing how Chinese enthusiasts innovate by combining European swordsmanship with ancient Chinese military manuals. The episode also follows the rise of BUHURT, a European-style armored combat sport now gaining traction in China, featuring locally made traditional Chinese armour in intense full-contact bouts. Through Kleber’s own multicultural martial arts studio and the city’s everyday East-West blend, the episode shows Shanghai as a global hub, a place where classical martial arts find new life and cross-cultural practices thrive.

programme.title: The Elegance of Luoyang
programme.desc: Luoyang, as the eastern starting point of the Silk Road, is a city with a rich history and a tradition of cultural inclusivity. As such, it enjoys a prominent position in the heritage of the whole world. In this episode, we’ll visit White Horse Temple to learn about its fascinating international background. At Longmen Grottoes, which were designated as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in the year 2000, we’ll appreciate how the unique ancient splendor is being revived by 3D technology. At the city’s annual Peony Festival, we’ll explore the significance of China’s national flower and national dress. Invited to a Luoyang Water Banquet, we’ll learn something about the city’s culinary traditions. And finally, a master of tang sancai, or tri-color pottery, will discuss the legacy of this ancient craft and its place in the modern era.

programme.title: China Bound: Wuhan
programme.desc: Tianran He visits Wuhan, a megacity at the crossroads of central China. From experiencing its vibrant breakfast culture and iconic dishes like hot dry noodles to exploring the lakes and hills of the expansive East Lake, as well as historic districts that showcase a blend of Chinese and Western architecture, Tianran uncovers why Wuhan has been a pivotal city in Chinese history for centuries. Finally, he relives some of these moments through immersive theater performances set amongst historic buildings and aboard a steamship cruising the Yangtze River.

programme.title: China Untapped: Xizang Ep.4
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He travels to Shannan, the cradle of Tibetan culture. Nestled in the gentlest valley of the Yarlung Zangbo River, this is where the first Tibetan king rose to power, the first palace in Xizang was built, and many other “firsts” in Xizang’s history began. Tianran visits Yamdrok Lake, one of Xizang’s three sacred lakes, where a paragliding base is taking shape. He then learns the art of Tibetan opera, joins rangers on a mission to track the elusive Tibetan red deer, and ends the day with a rejuvenating soak in a village revitalized by hot spring tourism.

programme.title: Young dancer of Batang melody
programme.desc: Tibetan girl Tashi Badro from Batang, Ganzi, Sichuan has learned how to do the Xianzi dance both from her mom and a school program. Batang Xianzi is a unique folk dance of the Tibetan ethnic group with a history spanning over 1,000 years on the Qinghai–Tibet Plateau. In order to keep the Tibetan tradition alive, Badro’s school has offered the dance as a cultural activity. Badro said dancing with her classmates is the happiest moment of her day. Watch the graceful movements of Badro and her peers performing the Xianzi dance.

programme.title: Foodwise: Changsha
programme.desc: In this episode, our hosts Tianran He and Eva May embark on a journey to Changsha in Hunan Province.?They taste their way through the local cuisine, discover the city's latest art and fashion trends, and find out why this place is one of China's "influencer hotspots", attracting millions of domestic and foreign tourists each year.

programme.title: Foodwise：Qingdao
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He and Eva May venture to Qingdao, China's beer mecca by the sea. With red tiles and green trees that make up the city's stunning scenery, our hosts embark on another foodie-journey with local delicacies and interesting guest appearances. From drinking beer with a German friend, catching crabs by the ocean and devouring grilled eel the classic Korean way, this episode will leave you hungry for more!

programme.title: Life as a Miao Embroidery Girl
programme.desc: In this episode, host Eva May travels to Maomaohe Village in Leishan County, Guizhou Province — a place renowned for its exquisite Miao embroidery. She spends a week immersing herself in the daily lives of local embroidery artisans, learning traditional Miao folk songs, and rising to the challenge of creating a piece of Miaoxiu embroidery with special cultural significance.

programme.title: The Story of Flying Trident
programme.desc: Feicha, or a flying trident, is a type of performance where you balance a revolving trident on your body. In Chinese history, the trident was one of the 18 main weapons of war, but now it has become more of an acrobatic act. For thousands of years, people in China have practiced it as a performance and also as a way to keep fit. It seems like the trident is stuck to the person’s body like a magnet, but that effect is achieved by tensing and relaxing muscles at the right time so that the movement of the trident is channeled in the right direction—be it around the hands, neck, or back. Otherwise, its energy will turn against the performer.

programme.title: Life in the Cloud
programme.desc: The second episode faithfully records what Tianran sees and feels during the backpacking tour in Danba County, Garze Tibetan Autonomous Prefecture, Sichuan Province. The perseverance, sincerity and hospitality of the people in Xizang leave a profound impression on the travelers visiting this land.

programme.title: Young dancer of Batang melody
programme.desc: Tibetan girl Tashi Badro from Batang, Ganzi, Sichuan has learned how to do the Xianzi dance both from her mom and a school program. Batang Xianzi is a unique folk dance of the Tibetan ethnic group with a history spanning over 1,000 years on the Qinghai–Tibet Plateau. In order to keep the Tibetan tradition alive, Badro’s school has offered the dance as a cultural activity. Badro said dancing with her classmates is the happiest moment of her day. Watch the graceful movements of Badro and her peers performing the Xianzi dance.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Five Ancestors Fist
programme.desc: In Quanzhou, southeast China, Kleber seeks out masters of the Five Ancestors Fist, a combination of five local kungfu styles. He meets the abbot of a Southern Shaolin Temple, and takes part in the extraordinary training regime used by Shaolin monks to hone their bodies and minds. He experiences the intricacies of Quanzhou’s traditional puppetry, and learns how Five Ancestors Fist incorporated military tactics that allowed its practitioners to band together and fight off pirates.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-4 Medieval Combat
programme.desc: Episode 4 centres on Shanghai, where Eastern and Western martial traditions meet in surprising ways. programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Five Ancestors Fist
programme.desc: In Quanzhou, southeast China, Kleber seeks out masters of the Five Ancestors Fist, a combination of five local kungfu styles. He meets the abbot of a Southern Shaolin Temple, and takes part in the extraordinary training regime used by Shaolin monks to hone their bodies and minds. He experiences the intricacies of Quanzhou’s traditional puppetry, and learns how Five Ancestors Fist incorporated military tactics that allowed its practitioners to band together and fight off pirates.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-5 Xingyi Quan
programme.desc: In Episode 5, Kleber travels to Shanxi, the birthplace of Xingyi Quan. He follows Master Cao Bing, a traditional Xingyi Quan teacher and modern combat coach, from Taiyuan’s noodle shops to Pingyao’s ancient streets, and from centuries-old vinegar breweries to Guandi temples, exploring Xingyi Quan’s deep links to Shanxi’s merchant culture and history. Master Cao breaks down the core principles of the “Five Elements and Twelve Forms”, and shows in his modern gym how techniques found in Muay Thai and Jiu-Jitsu can be integrated without losing Xingyi Quan’s philosophical core, guided by the mindset of taking only what is essential. Set against ancient sites such as Yongle Palace, this episode shows how true inner strength is like traditional timber architecture: stable, balanced, and built from within.

programme.title: World Heritage in China: Ancient City of Pingyao
programme.desc: Established in the 14th century, Pingyao retains the typical layout of the county-level cities of the Han people in Central China. The ancient city is a complete community comprising walls, streets and lanes, shops, houses and temples.?As a UNESCO World Heritage site, Pingyao remains a testament to traditional Chinese urban planning and architecture, attracting visitors from around the world who seek to explore its historical streets and vibrant history.

programme.title: China Bound: Harbin
programme.desc: Tianran He is in Harbin, the "Ice City", host of the 2025 Asian Winter Games. Internationally renowned for its breathtaking ice and snow sculptures, the city thrives with activity despite it -20°C winters. From savoring the famous local ice cream and hearty northeastern Chinese dishes such as stewed goose and sweet-and-sour pork, to drifting on the frozen Songhua River and carving down the slopse of Yabuli Ski Resort, discover how much fun you can have in Harbin.

programme.title: China Untapped: Xizang Ep.5
programme.desc: In this final episode, Tianran journeys to Nyingchi, known as “Xizang’s Garden.” Here, he experiences the region’s semi-pastoral way of life — feeding yaks, trying his hand at archery, and seeing how tourism is transforming lives in Lulang International Tourist Town. Travelling along National Highway 318, Tianran discovers towering millennia-old cypress trees and rafts through the vast Yani National Wetland Park, before ending the day with a unique spa treatment at a traditional Tibetan hospital.

programme.title: Face-changing wonder boy
programme.desc: Face-changing is an ancient Chinese performance art that is a component of Sichuan opera. Hu Yibo, 13, learned the act from his grandfather and other experienced masters. Dressed in brightly-colored costumes and while changing from one mask to another in just the blink of an eye, Yibo skillfully swipes open a fan to hide his "magical" movements. As a young inheritor, he undertakes daily training to perfect his skill set in the hope of becoming a master one day.

programme.title: Foodwise: Wuzhen
programme.desc: Join our hosts Tianran He and Eva May as they explore the boisterous event of Wuzhen's International Theatre festival, with colorful features of both theatrical and culinary nature. Foodie Tianran and art-lover Eva are accompanied by ubiquitous local street food, daily carnival performances and meet and greets with fascinating personalities. Let's set the stage for the beautiful merge of food and drama as they taste their way through the charming scene of Wuzhen.

programme.title: Foodwise：Eating Global in Beijing
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He and Eva May are in search of global cuisines that can be found in Beijing, the 3000-year old capital of China. From honest farm-to-table fare, to the culinary heights of French haute cuisine, and even a cooking class and wine and dine event at a traditional courtyard home in the city center, we'll discover that in Beijing, you can taste the world.

programme.title: Life as a Miao Silversmith
programme.desc: In this episode, Eva May steps into Maliao Village — a century-old silversmith village nestled in Leishan, Guizhou Province. Over the course of a week, she dives into the craft and daily lives of Miao men who carry on the silversmithing legacy. She works side by side with local silversmiths day in and day out, delving into the culture and techniques behind Miao silverware. She even joins master silversmiths for a live-stream event, before engaging in discussions with university professors about how traditional craftsmanship can fuel rural revitalization. On top of that, she witnesses the breathtaking sight of "maidens adorned in silver and flowers" at the grand Miao New Year celebrations, visits Xijiang Qianhu Miaozhai — the world’s largest Miao village — and learns to forge a small yet meaningful piece of Miao silver ornament.

programme.title: Tales in Light and Shadow
programme.desc: Beijing shadow puppets bring ancient Chinese tales to life with intricately crafted leather figures. Behind an illuminated screen, these colorful silhouettes dance, enacting epic stories and folklore accompanied by traditional music. Discover this captivating performing art, a cherished part of China's rich cultural tapestry and a mesmerizing visual spectacle.

programme.title: A Magic Chinese New Year
programme.desc: During the Year‑of‑the‑Ox Spring Festival, we visited Gubei Water Town in Miyun District, Beijing. Join host He Tianran to experience an authentic northern‑style Spring Festival. We will paste paper lanterns, cut paper‑cut window decorations, watch sugar‑blowing craftsmanship and release sky lanterns. Let us embrace this blessed Year of the Ox together.

programme.title: Face-changing wonder boy
programme.desc: Face-changing is an ancient Chinese performance art that is a component of Sichuan opera. Hu Yibo, 13, learned the act from his grandfather and other experienced masters. Dressed in brightly-colored costumes and while changing from one mask to another in just the blink of an eye, Yibo skillfully swipes open a fan to hide his "magical" movements. As a young inheritor, he undertakes daily training to perfect his skill set in the hope of becoming a master one day.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: White Crane
programme.desc: In Yongchun, China, Kleber explores the origins of White Crane kungfu and its effect on the martial arts world. He learns how its blocks and strikes were inspired by animal movements, and how acupuncture points can be used to deadly effect. After training his grip and finger strength with wooden sticks, he travels to Beijing for an introduction to Goju-ryu karate, and discovers striking similarities with White Crane.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-5 Xingyi Quan
programme.desc: In Episode 5, Kleber travels to Shanxi, the birthplace of Xingyi Quan. He follows Master Cao Bing, a traditional Xingyi Quan teacher and modern combat coach, from Taiyuan’s noodle shops to Pingyao’s ancient streets, and from centuries-old vinegar breweries to Guandi temples, exploring Xingyi Quan’s deep links to Shanxi’s merchant culture and history. Master Cao breaks down the core principles of the “Five Elements and Twelve Forms”, and shows in his modern gym how techniques found in Muay Thai and Jiu-Jitsu can be integrated without losing Xingyi Quan’s philosophical core, guided by the mindset of taking only what is essential. Set against ancient sites such as Yongle Palace, this episode shows how true inner strength is like traditional timber architecture: stable, balanced, and built from within.

programme.title: World Heritage in China: Ancient City of Pingyao
programme.desc: Established in the 14th century, Pingyao retains the typical layout of the county-level cities of the Han people in Central China. The ancient city is a complete community comprising walls, streets and lanes, shops, houses and temples.?As a UNESCO World Heritage site, Pingyao remains a testament to traditional Chinese urban planning and architecture, attracting visitors from around the world who seek to explore its historical streets and vibrant history.

programme.title: China Bound: Harbin
programme.desc: Tianran He is in Harbin, the "Ice City", host of the 2025 Asian Winter Games. Internationally renowned for its breathtaking ice and snow sculptures, the city thrives with activity despite it -20°C winters. From savoring the famous local ice cream and hearty northeastern Chinese dishes such as stewed goose and sweet-and-sour pork, to drifting on the frozen Songhua River and carving down the slopse of Yabuli Ski Resort, discover how much fun you can have in Harbin.

programme.title: China Untapped: Xizang Ep.5
programme.desc: In this final episode, Tianran journeys to Nyingchi, known as “Xizang’s Garden.” Here, he experiences the region’s semi-pastoral way of life — feeding yaks, trying his hand at archery, and seeing how tourism is transforming lives in Lulang International Tourist Town. Travelling along National Highway 318, Tianran discovers towering millennia-old cypress trees and rafts through the vast Yani National Wetland Park, before ending the day with a unique spa treatment at a traditional Tibetan hospital.

programme.title: The Lush Green Gannan Ep.2
programme.desc: This documentary captures the remarkable transformation of Gannan over the past decade, a region that serves as a critical water source and recharge zone for the upper reaches of the Yellow and Yangtze rivers, a national ecological functional area, and a pilot zone for ecological civilization.

programme.title: The Lush Green Gannan Ep.3
programme.desc: This documentary captures the remarkable transformation of Gannan over the past decade, a region that serves as a critical water source and recharge zone for the upper reaches of the Yellow and Yangtze rivers, a national ecological functional area, and a pilot zone for ecological civilization.

programme.title: Song Dynasty Poetry: Beauty Across Time Ep.4
programme.desc: Through the lens of Song dynasty poetry, this documentary brings to life the era's great poets, their works, and the stories behind them. By delving into their values and worldview, it reveals the deeper spirit within the culture, resulting in a work that is at once thoughtful, artistic, and deeply human.

programme.desc: To mark the 60th anniversary of the Xizang Autonomous Region, this informative documentary reveals its remarkable transformation through a comprehensive look at its economic, cultural and ecological progress over the past several decades.

programme.title: Razor in China·High-speed Train
programme.desc: From steam locomotives to intelligent high-speed rail, the development of China's railways is a story of continuous breakthroughs and innovation. Enter the world's most complex high-speed rail system and discover how China operates the largest high-speed railway network on earth.

programme.title: Back to the Far Side
programme.desc: Hear the stories of the Chang'e-6 engineers and four international payload teams and how they achieved their key mission milestones. Spanning China, France, the Netherlands, Sweden, Italy, and Pakistan, it offers a global perspective on humanity's first-ever mission to bring back samples from the far side of the moon.

programme.title: General Guan Yu Ep.1
programme.desc: This documentary is a unique exploration of the late Eastern Han dynasty general Guan Yu. Through a gaming team's recreation of the Battle of Xiangfan, overseas collections of his porcelain sculptures, and a coming-of-age ceremony at Guandi temple, this episode recounts his military achievements, while describing a man of loyalty, righteousness, courage, and honor—and an enduring spiritual symbol for the Chinese people.

programme.title: Foodwise: Haikou
programme.desc: Haikou, also known as "Coconut City," is famous for its coconut-infused cuisine. In this episode, Foodwise host Tianran heads to this coastal city to sample coconut chicken hotpot, fruity and spicy soups, silky Hainan pickled noodles, and check out Haikou’s famous local teahouses. Here, food and lifestyle go hand in hand—a perfect reflection of the city's slow, easy rhythm.

programme.title: Foodwise: Hainan's Four Famous Dishes
programme.desc: Follow Foodwise host Tianran as he tracks down Hainan’s four culinary treasures: Wenchang chicken, Jiaji duck, Dongshan goat, and Hele crab. He soon learns what makes these dishes so famous isn’t just their taste, it’s the animals themselves: chickens raised on coconuts, ducks that brave typhoons, cliff-grazing goats and crabs fattened in brackish waters—proving that in Hainan, the best dishes come with a story.

programme.title: Razor in China·Walk Again
programme.desc: This episode documents a groundbreaking surgery that uses technology to help those with spinal cord issues walk again. Hear from patients and doctors as they navigate the long road ahead, and the courage it takes to get there.

programme.title: Razor in China·Humanoids
programme.desc: From top-level design to application, from technical breakthroughs to ecosystem building—humanoid robots are rising at an unprecedented pace in China. But are they really that close to be part of our daily lives? The question is no longer "if," but “when”.

programme.desc: This documentary is a unique exploration of the late Eastern Han dynasty general Guan Yu. Through a gaming team's recreation of the Battle of Xiangfan, overseas collections of his porcelain sculptures, and a coming-of-age ceremony at Guandi temple, this episode recounts his military achievements, while describing a man of loyalty, righteousness, courage, and honor—and an enduring spiritual symbol for the Chinese people.

programme.title: Foodwise: Haikou
programme.desc: Haikou, also known as "Coconut City," is famous for its coconut-infused cuisine. In this episode, Foodwise host Tianran heads to this coastal city to sample coconut chicken hotpot, fruity and spicy soups, silky Hainan pickled noodles, and check out Haikou’s famous local teahouses. Here, food and lifestyle go hand in hand—a perfect reflection of the city's slow, easy rhythm.

programme.title: Foodwise: Hainan's Four Famous Dishes
programme.desc: Follow Foodwise host Tianran as he tracks down Hainan’s four culinary treasures: Wenchang chicken, Jiaji duck, Dongshan goat, and Hele crab. He soon learns what makes these dishes so famous isn’t just their taste, it’s the animals themselves: chickens raised on coconuts, ducks that brave typhoons, cliff-grazing goats and crabs fattened in brackish waters—proving that in Hainan, the best dishes come with a story.

programme.title: Razor in China·Walk Again
programme.desc: This episode documents a groundbreaking surgery that uses technology to help those with spinal cord issues walk again. Hear from patients and doctors as they navigate the long road ahead, and the courage it takes to get there.

programme.title: Razor in China·Humanoids
programme.desc: From top-level design to application, from technical breakthroughs to ecosystem building—humanoid robots are rising at an unprecedented pace in China. But are they really that close to be part of our daily lives? The question is no longer "if," but “when”.

programme.desc: This documentary reveals the current ecological state of the Yangtze River basin—including its fish, wildlife, and plant life—while documenting the efforts behind the Yangtze River protection initiative.

programme.title: Foodwise: The Taste of Home
programme.desc: In this episode, hosts Tianran and Eva return to their birthplaces—Beijing, China, and Vienna, Austria—to embark on a nostalgic culinary journey, revisiting the flavors of their past while sharing their impressions of their hometowns. From Vienna’s coffee culture to the teahouses in Beijing’s hutongs, every flavor carries a memory, and every meal tells a story of home.

programme.title: Foodwise: Shaoxing
programme.desc: In this episode, Foodwise host Tianran travels to Shaoxing, Zhejiang in search of the tastiest local flavors. From river delicacies and soy-scented cured foods to fermented dishes and the region's legendary yellow wine, Shaoxing’s bold flavors always leave a strong impression— proving that Jiangnan cuisine can be just as assertive as it is refined.

programme.title: Me and My Corps
programme.desc: This documentary offers an in-depth look into the lives of those living and working in the Xinjiang Production and Construction Corps. Divided into four chapters, it tells the stories of ordinary people—capturing their daily lives and the spirit of the era they embody.

programme.title: Cultural Inheritors Ep.1
programme.desc: From ancient texts to external therapies, from herbal medicine to processing techniques, the documentary Wind in the Apricot Grove explores the world of Traditional Chinese Medicine through eight aspects. Following more than a dozen practitioners, inheritors, and innovators, it traces the history, culture, and contemporary relevance of a tradition that continues to evolve.

programme.title: The Adventure in Powder Snow Ep.1
programme.desc: Centered on ice and snow sports and ice-snow cultural tourism, this documentary visits high-quality ski resorts and distinctive ice-snow destinations across China, presenting the development of the ice-snow industry and public ice-snow lifestyles. It records the experiences of ice-snow enthusiasts and the perseverance of practitioners, conveying the charm and vitality of ice-snow sports.

programme.title: Fe y Fuego
programme.desc: Dios nos restaura y unge para que el nombre de Jesús sea famoso. Jesús le dijo a la mujer en el pozo, "tu necesitas una fuente"; un manantial de agua está en constante movimiento por lo tanto no se estanca. Él es nuestra fuente de agua viva.

programme.title: Dios de Milagros
programme.desc: Experimente el poder de Dios en su vida y prepárese para recibir sanidad, restauración, y milagros mientras recibe una palabra llena de unción y poder predicada por el pastor Hernan Castaño desde la Iglesia Ríos De Aceite en Houston, Texas a las naciones. Su vida no será la misma! En Dios De Milagros, los enfermos son sanados, los cautivos liberados, los oprimidos ministrados, y todos crecen espiritualmente con las enseñanzas y predicaciones de los pastores Hernan y Wendy Castaño. Es tiempo de ver el mover de Dios en tu vida y en tu familia!

programme.title: Voz News
programme.desc: VOZ NEWS es el noticiero estelar de VOZ, con las principales noticias del país y del mundo, seguidas por un análisis en profundidad de temas destacados de la jornada. El ciclo es presentado por la reconocida periodista televisiva Karina Yapor, acompañada de un equipo de destacados periodistas y de analistas políticos, económicos y sociales.

programme.title: Sid Roth - ¡Es Sobrenatural!
programme.desc: Hal Sacks era un judío ortodoxo que filmó el Movimiento de Jesús, presenciando vidas transformadas por milagros, pero se negaba a creer. Entonces, un momento sobrenatural rompió su resistencia y le abrió los ojos a la verdad del Mesías.

programme.title: Joni Table Talk
programme.desc: Joni, un programa de entrevistas de media hora animado y, a veces, impredecible, aborda una amplia gama de temas relevantes, temas controvertidos y temas de actualidad con franqueza e ingenio. Combinando segmentos vibrantes con invitados notables.

programme.title: Ministry Now
programme.desc: Al frente de la nueva normalidad de hoy, la familia Lamb lleva a los espectadores de todo el mundo a Ministry Now. Lleno de fe radical, declaraciones alentadoras y consejos positivos, este programa le ayudará a cambiar su perspectiva hacia la esperanza que Dios aún tiene para su futuro.

programme.title: Aqua viva con Sergio Hornung
programme.desc: Aqua Viva presenta al Pastor Sergio Hornung cada semana compartiendo un mensaje cargado de fe, vision y herramientas practicas, a fin de experimentar una genuina transformación por el poder del Espíritu Santo.

programme.title: Impactando las Naciones
programme.desc: Impactando Naciones es un programa televisivo conducido por la Pastora Wanda Rolón, dedicado a compartir un mensaje transformador de fe, esperanza y poder espiritual y motivando a todo aquel que busca una experiencia real con Dios.

programme.title: Sin Miedo a Nada
programme.desc: El Pastor Iván Delgado te invita a vivir sin fronteras, a derribar obstáculos internos y externos, y a experimentar el poder de la Palabra como fuerza que transforma, sana y dirige el futuro.

programme.title: A Todo Gol
programme.desc: A Todo Gol une fútbol, fe y liderazgo. En el Mundial, invitados comparten análisis y reflexiones sobre valores de Dios fe y Cristo.

programme.title: Reflections
programme.desc: Disfruta de impresionantes escenas de muchas de las creaciones milagrosas de Dios. Himnos familiares y música original bellamente producida acompañan las Escrituras para ayudarlo a dejarse llevar y rendirse durante un tiempo de oración y meditación diaria

programme.title: Jentezen Franklin
programme.desc: Encuentre respuestas en la Palabra de Dios y aprenda a vivir cada día con un propósito y significado mientras Jentezen Franklin presenta la verdad clara y simple que lo inspirará a ser lo mejor posible.

programme.title: Pastor Sam Rodriguez
programme.desc: Con un mensaje que inspira, eleva y activa el propósito de Dios en cada persona y familia, el pastor Samuel Rodriguez comparte la palabra del señor con enseñanzas, ilustraciones, y verdades que desatan la gloria de Dios en esta generación.

programme.title: Joni Table Talk
programme.desc: ¡Ya no dejes que la vida te derrote! Samuel Rodríguez comparte cómo liberarte de la duda y el miedo para cumplir tu propósito y vivir en una libertad más profunda.

programme.title: Andrés Spyker de Más Vida
programme.desc: Conéctate cada semana con un mensaje fresco de la palabra de Dios con una aplicación práctica para tu vida.

programme.title: Dios de Milagros
programme.desc: Experimente el poder de Dios en su vida y prepárese para recibir sanidad, restauración, y milagros mientras recibe una palabra llena de unción y poder predicada por el pastor Hernan Castaño desde la Iglesia Ríos De Aceite en Houston, Texas a las naciones. Su vida no será la misma! En Dios De Milagros, los enfermos son sanados, los cautivos liberados, los oprimidos ministrados, y todos crecen espiritualmente con las enseñanzas y predicaciones de los pastores Hernan y Wendy Castaño. Es tiempo de ver el mover de Dios en tu vida y en tu familia!

programme.title: Andrew Wommack La Verdad Del Evangelio
programme.desc: Andrew Wommack comparte cómo la Palabra de Dios transformó su vida. Él cree que el mismo poder puede obrar para ti. Su enseñanza es profunda, clara y tan simple que hasta un niño puede entenderla.

programme.title: Jason Lozano
programme.desc: La misión de Jason Lozano Ministries es llevar el mensaje de libertad a las naciones. A través de ganar almas y hacer discípulos podemos ayudar a las naciones a hacer una vida de libertad.

programme.title: Sid Roth - ¡Es Sobrenatural!
